chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ وَ الْعُمْرَةِ وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Merits of the Hajj and the Umra, and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/136)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/136)
text: إِذَا أَخَذَ النَّاسُ مَوَاطِنَهُمْ بِمِنًى نَادَى مُنَادٍ مِنْ قِبَلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِنْ أَرَدْتُمْ أَنْ أَرْضَى فَقَدْ رَضِيتُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hajjal, from Dawood Bin Abu Yazeed,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When the people take their places in Mina, a Caller calls out on behalf of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic: ‘If you are intending for Me<sup>azwj</sup> to be Pleased, so I<sup>asws</sup> am Pleased’.
en.sarwar: 41. Ibn abu ‘Umayr has narrated from Hisham ibn al-Hakam who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that no other journey affects one’s flesh, blood, skin and hair more than the journey to Makkah. No one reaches it (the journey) without facing hardships.’”
